entries.title: Caamas Document Crisis Is a Political Flashpoint

content.fact: The Caamas Document crisis threatens to fracture the New Republic through old Imperial atrocity, Bothan culpability, and factional outrage.
content.injection: During the Caamas crisis, emphasize political legitimacy, species grievances, manipulated evidence, and the danger of civil fragmentation.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Caamas Document Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Caamas Document Crisis to Caamas Document Crisis.
antiLore: Do not skip ahead to later character, faction, relationship, location, or event states.

entries.title: Bothans Face Political Pressure After Caamas

content.fact: Bothan involvement in the Caamas tragedy creates enormous political pressure and resentment inside the New Republic.
content.injection: In Caamas-era scenes, write Bothan politics as defensive, strategic, and vulnerable to backlash rather than neutral background.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Caamas Document Crisis.
Respect story-position bounds from Caamas Document Crisis to Caamas Document Crisis.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Thrawn Return Rumor Destabilizes the Galaxy

content.fact: The possibility of Thrawn’s return becomes a destabilizing rumor and strategic lever before the truth is resolved.
content.injection: During Hand of Thrawn context, use uncertainty over Thrawn’s return as pressure; do not immediately reveal the full truth unless the context has reached Nirauan resolution.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Hand of Thrawn / Imperial peace.
Respect story-position bounds from Caamas Document Crisis to New Republic and Imperial Remnant Peace.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Nirauan and the Hand of Thrawn Are Hidden

content.fact: Nirauan and the Hand of Thrawn site conceal major Chiss/Imperial-linked secrets until discovered.
content.injection: Before the Hand of Thrawn resolution, do not let ordinary characters know the site or its truth; treat it as high-level hidden strategic lore.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Hand of Thrawn Resolution.
Respect story-position bounds from Hand of Thrawn Resolution to Hand of Thrawn Resolution.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

context.label: New Republic and Imperial Remnant Peace

content.fact: Pellaeon’s later Imperial leadership favors survival, order, and negotiated peace over endless war against the New Republic.
content.injection: In Hand of Thrawn-era scenes, write Pellaeon as a pragmatic Remnant statesman-soldier, not merely a nostalgic Imperial attacker.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: New Republic and Imperial Remnant Peace.
Respect story-position bounds from New Republic and Imperial Remnant Peace to New Republic and Imperial Remnant Peace.

entries.title: Imperial Peace Treaty Changes Galactic State

context.label: New Republic and Imperial Remnant Peace

content.fact: The New Republic and Imperial Remnant peace settlement changes the galactic conflict from existential civil war to a smaller recognized Remnant state.
content.injection: After the Pellaeon-Gavrisom peace, do not write the Imperial Remnant as the same all-consuming warfront it was immediately after Endor.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: New Republic and Imperial Remnant Peace.
Respect story-position bounds from New Republic and Imperial Remnant Peace to New Republic and Imperial Remnant Peace.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.

entries.title: Luke and Mara Relationship Changes After Hand of Thrawn

content.fact: Luke and Mara’s relationship transforms decisively during the Hand of Thrawn events, leading toward their engagement and future marriage.
content.injection: Before Hand of Thrawn, avoid writing Luke and Mara as settled partners; after it, allow explicit romantic commitment and shared future planning.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: Hand of Thrawn Resolution.
Respect story-position bounds from Hand of Thrawn Resolution to Hand of Thrawn Resolution.

entries.title: Hand of Thrawn Closes the Bantam-Era War State

context.label: New Republic and Imperial Remnant Peace

content.fact: The Hand of Thrawn resolution functions as a major closure point for the earlier post-Endor Imperial war state before the Yuuzhan Vong era.
content.injection: Use the Hand of Thrawn as a transition anchor: after it, the galaxy is closer to uneasy peace and pre-NJO setup than constant Imperial assault.
constraints: Apply only when the active Saga Context overlaps: New Republic and Imperial Remnant Peace.
Respect story-position bounds from New Republic and Imperial Remnant Peace to New Republic and Imperial Remnant Peace.
antiLore: Do not treat later-era, alternate-continuity, or fanon facts as true in this Context unless the user explicitly selected that branch.
